channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [INFOMEDIA] Infomedia Press Board Approves Q4/FY26 Results; Going Concern Uncertainty
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Infomedia Press Ltd's Board of Directors approved the Audited Financial Results for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The Statutory Auditors issued an unmodified opinion on the results. However, the auditor's report highlights a material uncertainty regarding the company's ability to continue as a going concern, citing a net loss of ₹300.20 lakh for FY26 and accumulated losses of ₹11,287.28 lakh, resulting in negative net worth. This uncertainty is mitigated by a support letter from the parent company, Network 18 Media & Investments Limited, assuring financial support for the foreseeable future.

  • Board approved Audited Financial Results for Q4 and FY ended March 31, 2026.
  • Auditors issued an unmodified opinion on the financial results.
  • Company reported a net loss of ₹300.20 lakh for FY26.
  • Accumulated losses total ₹11,287.28 lakh, leading to negative net worth.
  • Parent company, Network 18, provided a support letter for going concern.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: INFOMEDIA PRESS LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [IDEA] Vodafone Idea fined ₹21.46 Lacs by TRAI for non-compliance
item.description:
Vodafone Idea Ltd has received an order from the Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) levying a financial disincentive of ₹21,46,000. The order, dated April 17, 2026, is under the Telecom Commercial Communications Customer Preference Regulations, 2018. The penalty stems from the company's failure to implement/enforce a scrubbing mechanism and non-compliance with provisions related to unsolicited commercial communication for the quarter ending June 2024. Vodafone Idea is currently reviewing the order and evaluating its next steps. The maximum financial impact is limited to the disincentive amount.

  • TRAI imposed a ₹21.46 Lacs financial disincentive on Vodafone Idea.
  • Penalty is for non-compliance with commercial communication regulations.
  • Violations include failure to implement scrubbing mechanism in Q1 FY25.
  • Company is reviewing the order and evaluating next steps.
  • Maximum financial impact is limited to the disincentive amount.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Vodafone Idea Ltd | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [WITS] Wherrelz IT Solutions FY26 Results, Capital Hike & Preferential Issue
item.description:
Wherrelz IT Solutions Ltd's Board of Directors approved the audited financial results for the half year and year ended March 31, 2026. The board also proposed a significant increase in the authorized share capital from ₹50 lakhs to ₹48 crores, subject to member approval. Additionally, the company plans to issue up to 4,67,70,300 equity shares of ₹10 each to non-promoters on a preferential basis, for the conversion of outstanding unsecured loans. An Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) is scheduled for May 18, 2026, to seek shareholder approval for these proposals.

  • Board approved audited financial results for FY26.
  • Authorized share capital to increase from ₹50 lakhs to ₹48 crores.
  • Preferential issue of 4.67 crore shares to non-promoters at ₹10 each.
  • Issue is for conversion of outstanding unsecured loans into equity.
  • EGM scheduled for May 18, 2026, to approve capital changes and issue.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Wherrelz IT Solutions Ltd | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [WITS] Wherrelz IT Board Approves FY26 Results, ₹48 Cr Capital Hike, Preferential Issue
item.description:
Wherrelz IT Solutions Ltd's Board of Directors approved the Audited Financial Results for the half year and year ended March 31, 2026. The board also proposed to increase the authorized share capital from ₹50 lakhs to ₹48 crores, subject to member approval. Additionally, it approved a preferential issue of up to 4,67,70,300 equity shares of ₹10 each to non-promoters, for converting outstanding unsecured loans into shares, also subject to member and appropriate authority approvals. An Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) is scheduled for May 18, 2026, to seek member approvals for these proposals.

  • Board approved Audited Financial Results for FY26.
  • Proposed authorized share capital increase from ₹50 lakhs to ₹48 crores.
  • Approved preferential issue of 4.67 Cr shares to non-promoters for loan conversion.
  • EGM scheduled for May 18, 2026, to seek member approvals for capital changes and preferential issue.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Wherrelz IT Solutions Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [NTPCGREEN] NTPC Green: 87.5 MW Solar Project in Rajasthan Declared Commercial
item.description:
🔥 Trending

NTPC Green Energy Limited announced that 87.50 MW of its 150 MW solar project in Rajasthan has been declared commercially operational as of April 19, 2026. This capacity addition comes from Project Eleven Renewable Power Private Limited, a step-down subsidiary of the ONGC NTPC Green Private Limited joint venture. With this new capacity, the total installed capacity for the NTPC Green Energy Limited Group increases from 10,276.40 MW to 10,363.90 MW.

  • 87.5 MW solar capacity in Rajasthan declared commercially operational.
  • Project is a step-down subsidiary of ONGC NTPC Green JV.
  • Commercial operation effective from April 19, 2026.
  • NTPC Green Group's total installed capacity now 10,363.90 MW.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: NTPC Green Energy Ltd | Category: Project Update

item.title: [SECURCRED] SecUR Credentials Q1 FY25 Unaudited Standalone Financial Results Approved
item.description:
SecUR Credentials Ltd's Board of Directors, in a meeting held on April 17, 2026, considered and approved the unaudited standalone financial results for the quarter ended June 30, 2024. The results were accompanied by a Limited Review Report from the Statutory Auditors, JPMD & ASSOCIATES. The auditors conducted their review in accordance with Standard on Review Engagements (SRE) 2410 and concluded that, based on their procedures, nothing came to their attention that would suggest the financial statements contain any material misstatement or fail to disclose required information, except for those mentioned in the notes to accounts. The Audit Committee had previously reviewed these results.

  • Board approved unaudited standalone financial results for Q1 FY25.
  • Results for the quarter ended June 30, 2024, were considered and approved.
  • Statutory Auditors provided a Limited Review Report for the period.
  • Auditors found no material misstatement in the financial results.
  • The Audit Committee reviewed and the Board of Directors approved the financials.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SecUR Credentials Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [OROSMITHS] Orosil Smiths leases property in Noida for 11 months
item.description:
Orosil Smiths India Ltd has informed that it has taken a property on lease situated at Basement Floor, Property No. A-18, Sector-6, Noida, District Gautam Budh Nagar, Uttar Pradesh. The lease agreement is for a period of eleven months and is stated to be in the ordinary course of business for the company.

  • Orosil Smiths leased a property in Noida, Uttar Pradesh.
  • The lease agreement is for an eleven-month period.
  • The leasing activity is considered part of ordinary business operations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: OROSIL SMITHS INDIA LTD.-$ | Category: Business Update

item.title: [GUJGAS] MCA sanctions Gujarat Gas Composite Scheme of Arrangement
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Gujarat Gas Ltd (GGL) has announced the receipt of the final order from the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) sanctioning its Composite Scheme of Arrangement. This scheme involves Gujarat State Petroleum Corporation Limited (GSPC), Gujarat State Petronet Limited (GSPL), GSPC Energy Limited (GEL) as Transferor Companies, Gujarat Gas Limited (GGL) as the Transferee/Demerged Company, and GSPL Transmission Limited (GTL) as the Resulting Company. The arrangement, governed by Sections 230-232 of the Companies Act, 2013, facilitates a significant corporate restructuring for the involved entities. The final order from the MCA was issued on April 8, 2026, and a copy has been uploaded to the company's website.

  • MCA sanctioned Gujarat Gas's Composite Scheme of Arrangement.
  • The scheme involves GSPC, GSPL, GEL, GGL, and GSPL Transmission.
  • This approval enables the planned corporate restructuring.
  • The final order from MCA was received today, dated April 8, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Gujarat Gas Ltd | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [SHARDFI] Eco Hotels: Rights Issue Funds Utilized, ₹187.35 Lakhs Reallocated
item.description:
Eco Hotels And Resorts Ltd reported on the utilization of funds from its partly paid-up Rights Issue for the quarter ended March 31, 2026. The company raised ₹1957.27 Lakhs, with ₹825.77 Lakhs received from application and first call money. While there is no overall deviation in the use of funds, the company reallocated ₹187.35 Lakhs from identified property advances to investment in new, unidentified properties for construction/renovation and security deposits. This was due to delays in requirements for originally identified properties. Additionally, actual general corporate expenses increased compared to initial plans.

  • Eco Hotels reported on Rights Issue fund utilization for Q4 FY26.
  • ₹825.77 Lakhs received from the partly paid-up Rights Issue.
  • ₹187.35 Lakhs reallocated from identified to unidentified properties.
  • Reallocation due to delays in original property requirements.
  • No overall deviation in fund use, but internal object interchange.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SHARAD FIBRES & YARN PROCESSORS LTD. | Category: Rights Issue

item.title: [SECURCRED] SecUR Credentials Board Approves Q1 FY25 Unaudited Results
item.description:
The Board of Directors of SecUR Credentials Ltd, in a meeting held on April 17, 2026, approved the unaudited standalone financial results for the quarter ended June 30, 2024. The company also attached the Limited Review Report from its Statutory Auditors, JPMD & ASSOCIATES, for the corresponding period, in compliance with SEBI Listing Regulations. The financial results were reviewed by the Audit Committee prior to Board approval.

  • Board approved unaudited standalone financial results for Q1 FY25.
  • Meeting held on April 17, 2026, to consider and approve results.
  • Results pertain to the quarter ended June 30, 2024.
  • Limited Review Report from Statutory Auditors, JPMD & ASSOCIATES, was attached.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SecUR Credentials Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result
